intent.description: How the workspace plans to use this domain. Drives which DNS records the cron treats as required (and so the auto-flip to active).

localPart.description: Local part of the address (portion before @)

data.description: Sample data to render variables with.

points.description: Dense day-bucketed counts for the requested window. Days with zero messages still appear with `count: 0`.

data.description: Sample data to render variables with.
Data object to render into the template

messages.description: Up to 100 messages to render and enqueue in a single call. Each is processed independently — partial successes are allowed.

data.description: Template data passed into the Handlebars render.

from.description: From address. Must be authorized to send on its domain.

idempotency_key.description: Optional per-row idempotency key. Same dedupe namespace as the `Idempotency-Key` request header.

subject.description: Optional override for the rendered subject. Falls back to the template subject.

data.description: Data object to render into the template

subject.description: Subject override (optional; uses template subject otherwise)

to.description: To email address or list of addresses

data.description: Variable values to render into the template. Keys correspond to the dotted paths returned by the /variables endpoint.

from.description: From email address. Must be on a verified domain owned by the workspace, same as a normal send.

to.description: Recipient address for the test email. Suppression-list and rate-limit checks are bypassed for test sends, so a recently-bounced address is still reachable for QA.

count.description: Count of messages in this bucket.

backgroundColor.description: Page background — the area outside the email card.

brandName.description: Display name shown in the email header (falls back to your workspace display name if not set).

buttonRadius.description: CSS length value for button border-radius (e.g. "6px", "0", "9999px" for fully round).

containerMaxWidth.description: Maximum width of the email card. Bulletproof tables max-out at this.

extras.description: Extra key/value tokens addressable as {{brand.extras.<key>}} in templates. Use for marketing strings, secondary accent colors, etc.

fontStack.description: CSS font-family stack. Email clients can't use webfonts reliably — list system fonts in fallback order.

footerAddress.description: Postal address line for compliance footers (CAN-SPAM, etc.). Plain text, displayed verbatim.

footerHtml.description: Custom HTML to render in the email footer. Inline styles only — no <style> tags. Used by templates that opt into `{{> bp.footer}}`.

logoUrl.description: Public URL of your logo. Recommended: PNG/SVG, ≤200px tall, hosted on a domain you control or any CDN reachable at send time.

mutedTextColor.description: Muted text color for footers + secondary copy.

primaryColor.description: Primary brand color. Used for buttons, links, and accent strokes. Hex string (e.g. #2d29d7).

secondaryColor.description: Secondary brand color, typically darker than primary. Used for hover states + secondary accents.

socialLinks.description: Map of social-platform → URL. Recognized keys: twitter, x, linkedin, github, instagram, facebook, youtube, tiktok, mastodon, bluesky, web.

dnsProvider.description: Identifier from the wizard's provider list. Free-form so we can ship new entries without a migration. NULL clears the choice.

url.description: Destination URL. Must be absolute https:// with a public TLD.

fromEmail.description: Default From email address. Must be authorized to send on the chosen domain.

fromName.description: Default From name on outbound messages.

password.description: SMTP password. Stored encrypted at rest; never returned by GET endpoints.

port.description: SMTP server port (commonly 587 for STARTTLS, 465 for implicit TLS).

secure.description: Whether to use implicit TLS (true → port 465 style). For STARTTLS on 587, set false.

username.description: SMTP username. May be omitted alongside password for anonymous SMTP (e.g. postfix on a private network); otherwise both are required.

kind.description: Kind: "template" (sendable email; default), "layout" (chrome wrapper referenced via {{> layout.<name>}}), "partial" (reusable block referenced via {{> partial.<name>}}). Cannot be changed after create — delete + recreate to switch kinds.

name.description: Unique template name within the app (lowercase letters, digits, underscore, hyphen)

subject.description: Subject line (Handlebars). Layouts and partials ignore this field — they are not standalone-sendable. Pass an empty string for those.

tags.description: Workspace-scoped labels for organizing templates. Free-form strings; the editor surfaces existing tags as autocomplete.

variablesSchema.description: Declared variable schema. Shape: `{version:1, vars:{<name>:{type, required?, default?, options?}}}`. When set, render + send validate the payload against the schema and 400 with descriptive errors on mismatch.

fromEmail.description: Default From email address. Must be authorized to send on the chosen domain.

fromName.description: Default From name on outbound messages.

password.description: SMTP password. Stored encrypted at rest; never returned by GET endpoints.

port.description: SMTP server port (commonly 587 for STARTTLS, 465 for implicit TLS).

secure.description: Whether to use implicit TLS (true → port 465 style). For STARTTLS on 587, set false.

username.description: SMTP username. May be omitted alongside password for anonymous SMTP (e.g. postfix on a private network); otherwise both are required.

post.summary: Inbound bounce webhook for the workspace-default SMTP config. Public; authenticates via URL secret + provider signature.
Inbound bounce webhook for a per-domain SMTP override. Same auth as the workspace-default URL but tagged with the domain so the audit log attributes correctly.

delete.summary: Remove all brand customizations and revert to platform defaults.

get.summary: Get the resolved brand tokens for the workspace. Always returns a complete object, with platform defaults filling in any unset fields.

put.summary: Replace the workspace brand. Any field omitted from the body is reset to the platform default for that field. To partially-update, send back what GET returned with the desired diff applied.

get.summary: List domains registered to this workspace

post.summary: Register a domain for a workspace

409.description: Domain has existing mailboxes; delete them first

patch.summary: Update user-side domain metadata (today: dnsProvider; the wizard saves the user's provider choice here so hostnames render correctly across reloads).

delete.summary: Drop the per-domain override; sends from this domain fall back to the workspace default (or shared relay).

get.summary: Get the per-domain SMTP override (returns null if unset; the workspace default applies in that case).

put.summary: Create or update the per-domain SMTP override (resets verification)

post.summary: Test the per-domain SMTP override; updates status

403.description: Caller lacks `mailbox.read` permission, or workspace has not enabled the `envoi` service.

get.summary: List mailboxes in this workspace. 20 per page by default (max 200) — paginate with the cursor returned in `pagination.next_cursor` while `pagination.has_more` is true.

400.description: Validation failure (invalid local part, retention out of range).

403.description: Caller lacks `mailbox.create` permission, or workspace has not enabled the `envoi` service.

404.description: Domain not found in this workspace.

409.description: Mailbox local-part already exists on this domain.

post.summary: Create a mailbox under a verified domain. Local-part + domain combination must be globally unique.

404.description: Mailbox not found in this workspace.

delete.summary: Soft-delete a mailbox. The row is marked deleted and stops accepting new mail; existing messages are kept until retention purges them.

404.description: Mailbox not found in this workspace.

400.description: Validation failure or invalid status transition.

404.description: Mailbox not found in this workspace.

patch.summary: Update mailbox display name, retention, or status. Status transitions are restricted (active ↔ suspended; archived is terminal).

parameters.description: Only include messages received on or after this ISO date
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Only include messages received on or before this ISO date
parameters.name: until

parameters.description: Filter to only unread (true) or only read (false) messages
parameters.name: unread

get.summary: Stream a single attachment by its position
Stream the raw RFC5322 .eml for a message

get.description: Returns dense day buckets in [since, until). `metric=sent` (default) reads received messages; `metric=bounced` reads MX-time rejections matched to this workspace by envelope-to.

parameters.description: Bucket size. Only `day` is supported today; the param is here so future hour/week additions don’t break clients.
parameters.name: granularity

parameters.description: Inclusive start of the window (ISO-8601). Treated in UTC for bucket alignment.
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Which counter to bucket. `sent` (default) reads from received messages; `bounced` reads from MX-time rejections matching this workspace's mailbox addresses.
parameters.name: metric

get.summary: Day-bucketed message volume for a workspace

delete.summary: Revert the workspace to the shared SMTP relay

get.summary: Get the workspace-default SMTP config (returns null if unset). Per-domain overrides live under /workspaces/:id/domains/:domainId/smtp-config.

put.summary: Create or update the workspace-default SMTP config (resets verification)

post.summary: Test arbitrary SMTP creds without persisting
Test the persisted workspace-default SMTP config; updates status

get.summary: List every SMTP config in the workspace (default + per-domain overrides)

post.summary: Add an email to the workspace suppression list

get.summary: List templates / layouts / partials in a workspace. Filter via ?kind=template|layout|partial (default: all). Filter by tags via ?tags=foo,bar (comma-separated).

post.summary: Lint a draft for deliverability red flags. Same render pipeline as /preview; returns a 0-100 score (higher is better) and a sorted list of findings (errors first). Self-developed heuristic linter, no external classifier — runs in-process.
Preview an unsaved draft. Renders subject + bodyHtml against the workspace brand tokens + supplied data, with all workspace layouts/partials registered. Used by the editor for live preview.

get.summary: Distinct tag list across all templates in this workspace (for autocomplete)

parameters.description: Opaque client-generated string (1–256 chars, `[A-Za-z0-9_-:]`) used to deduplicate retries within a 24h window.
parameters.name: Idempotency-Key

404.description: Template not found in this workspace.

post.summary: Send a template-rendered message. Pass an `Idempotency-Key` header (1..256 chars, [A-Za-z0-9_\-:]) to make retries safe — replays return the original response with `Idempotent-Replay: true` for 24h. Same key + different request body returns 409 IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REUSE.

200.description: Batch accepted; each row reports its own status.

400.description: Validation failure (malformed body, batch too large).

404.description: Template not found in this workspace.

post.summary: Send up to 100 messages with the same template in one request. Per-row partial-success: each row carries its own status (`accepted` | `rejected`). Pass `idempotency_key` per row to make retries safe — same Redis namespace as the request-header path on /send.

Send a test rendering of the template to an arbitrary recipient. Bypasses suppression-list and rate-limit checks; subject is auto-prefixed with [TEST]; missing variables render as [varname] placeholders.

get.summary: Variables auto-detected from the saved template body + subject (Handlebars expressions, dotted paths preserved, dedup'd in first-occurrence order).

List the workspace default + every per-domain override. Plaintext signing secrets are NOT returned — only the last-4-char hint. Mint or rotate via the secret endpoints to receive the plaintext (once).
Recent delivery attempts across all of this workspace's webhook configs. Includes status code, latency, response body (truncated to 1KB), and error if any.

put.summary: Create or replace the workspace-default outbound webhook. Returns the plaintext signing secret ONCE — store it now.
